title: 10 best herbal decoctions for nasal congestion
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for nasal congestion are a natural remedy made by steeping herbs in hot water to release their medicinal properties, which helps to soothe and clear congested nasal passages. These decoctions work by reducing inflammation, loosening mucus, and opening up airways, providing instant relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with nasal congestion include peppermint, eucalyptus, and ginger, which can be made into teas or taken as capsules. By using these decoctions, individuals can breathe easier, sleep better, and enjoy daily activities without the discomfort of nasal congestion, significantly improving their quality of life.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Purple coneflower's decoction has been shown to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and ease breathing.

Antiviral properties: The decoction contains compounds that have been found to inhibit the replication of certain viruses that can cause respiratory tract infections, including the common cold and sinusitis, which are common causes of nasal congestion.
Expectorant activity: Purple coneflower's decoction has a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with nasal congestion because it contains bioactive compounds that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the decoction's antimicrobial properties help to combat infections that can cause congestion. By reducing inflammation and combating infection, purple coneflower decoctions provide natural relief from nasal congestion and promote healthy respiratory function.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help with nasal congestion by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.

Phenolic acids (including chlorogenic acid): These compounds help with nasal congestion by possessing anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties that help to alleviate nasal congestion and reduce mucus production.

Alkylamides: These compounds help with nasal congestion by exhibiting antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce nasal congestion, itching, and sneezing associated with allergies and colds.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea roots are used in decoctions for nasal congestion because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which can help alleviate congestion and soothe the nasal passages.

Leaves: Echinacea leaves are used in decoctions for nasal congestion because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and promote healing in the nasal area.

Stems: Echinacea stems are used in decoctions for nasal congestion because they contain glycosides, phenolic acids, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Chop 3-4 grams of fresh echinacea purpurea root into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped echinacea root with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Steep the echinacea mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the echinacea decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum decoctions helps with nasal congestion because its unique combination of eucalyptol, borneol, and camphor creates a powerful expectorant effect. These natural compounds loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the decongestant properties of Tasmanian blue gum help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, further alleviating congestion. As a result, regular consumption of these decoctions can provide relief from stubborn nasal congestion, allowing for improved respiratory function and overall comfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum has antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties that help combat infections causing nasal congestion, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, reduce swelling in the nasal passages, ease congestion, and alleviate sinus pressure.
Expectorant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps loosen and expel mucus, making it easier to breathe and clear out congested airways.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: A terpene that acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax the muscles in the airways and reduce inflammation, which in turn helps to alleviate nasal congestion.
α-Pinene: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, thereby relieving nasal congestion and cough.

Catechin: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, which in turn helps to alleviate nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves contain high levels of eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps to relieve nasal congestion.
Barks: The barks of Eucalyptus globulus contain various bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease respiratory congestion.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus contain eucalyptol and other compounds that help to thin mucus and provide relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and store them in an airtight container.
Measure out 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil in a saucepan over high heat.
Add the eucalyptus globulus leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to a simmer.
Simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the nasal passages. The decoction's glycyrrhizin component also has expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, licorice's natural antiviral and antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to congestion. By drinking a warm licorice decoction, individuals can experience rapid relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion.
Mucolytic properties: The saponins present in licorice root have mucolytic properties, which break down excess mucus in the nasal passages, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial compounds: Licorice root contains antiviral and antibacterial compounds like glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, which help combat infections that can cause nasal congestion and sinusitis.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: As a potent anti-inflammatory compound, glycyrrhizin helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid compound has been shown to exhibit expectorant properties, which aid in the removal of mucus and other debris from the nasal passages, helping to relieve congestion.
Licoricidin: With its bronchodilatory and anti-inflammatory effects, licoricidin helps to relax the smooth muscle in the airways and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe through the nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are commonly used to make decoctions for nasal congestion due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make decoctions, as they contain flavonoids and saponins that help in reducing nasal congestion and inflammation.

Barks: The barks are another part of the plant used to make decoctions, containing compounds that help in relieving nasal congestion and coughs due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 2 liters of water for decoction.
Combine the dried roots in a saucepan with the water and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool completely before storing it in the refrigerator for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with nasal congestion because its natural oils and compounds, such as menthol and camphor, have a soothing and decongesting effect on the nasal passages. When inhaled, these compounds help to thin mucus, reduce inflammation, and relax the smooth muscles lining the nasal tract, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness. Additionally, peppermint's cooling properties can also help to calm and open up constricted airways, further aiding in the relief of congestion.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Peppermint's decoction helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure.

Natural decongestant properties: The menthol content in peppermint decoction helps to thin out mucus, making it less sticky and easier to expel from the nasal passages, providing quick relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a terpene that helps thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve nasal congestion.
Menthone: Menthone is another terpene that exhibits expectorant properties, which help to clear mucus from the airways and relieve nasal congestion.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, thereby relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Highly aromatic and volatile oils in the leaves are released when made into a decoction, providing relief from nasal congestion.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita contain menthone, a compound that helps to ease congestion and open airways.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used to make decoctions, releasing menthol and other oils that help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and stems from a clean outdoor environment.
Clean and chop the harvested leaves and stems into small pieces for easier infusion.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the chopped leaves and stems in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids from the infusion.
Cool the liquid to room temperature and store it in an airtight container for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages. The antibacterial and antifungal compounds in thyme also help to combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to congestion. Additionally, the decongestant properties of thyme can reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal tissues, providing quick relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Thyme's expectorant property helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving nasal congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to sinus pressure and congestion.
Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Thyme's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to nasal congestion, such as colds or sinusitis.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby relieving congestion.
Carvacrol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to congestion.
Bornyl acetate: Demonstrates decongestant properties, helping to reduce the production of mucus and ease congestion by thinning and draining excess mucus from the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of thymol, a compound that has decongestant and antimicrobial properties.
Stems: The stems are used to make decoctions that help to relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and promoting drainage.
Buds: The buds are used to make decoctions that help to ease nasal congestion and coughs, thanks to their high content of thymol and other antiseptic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean the plant material by gently brushing off dirt and debris to prevent contamination.
Combine the cleaned thymus vulgaris with 1 liter of boiling water and allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the solid plant material.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural expectorant properties. The herb contains compounds such as thujone, camphor, and borneol that help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, providing quick relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, further easing congestion. By promoting drainage and reducing inflammation, sage decoctions offer a natural solution for relieving nasal congestion and promoting easier breathing.
remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Sage's essential oils and flavonoids help combat viral and bacterial infections that cause nasal congestion, reducing inflammation and clearing out mucus.
Expectorant properties: Sage's saponins and phenolic acids help loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's polyphenols and flavonoids reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which helps alleviate swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with congestion.
remedy_constituents: Ternenoid triterpenes (e.g., ursolic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.

Rosmarinic acid (a phenolic diterpene): As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, rosmarinic acid can help alleviate nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal tissues.
Carvacrol (a phenolic monoterpenoid): Carvacrol's decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties enable it to help clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids that have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain sesquiterpene compounds that can help in reducing nasal congestion and inflammation.
Buds: Salvia officinalis buds are used in decoctions due to their rich content of eucalyptol, which is a natural decongestant and expectorant that helps relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 to 15 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves for at least 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the dried salvia officinalis powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes then strain and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with nasal congestion because it contains a unique compound called anethole, which has natural expectorant properties. When inhaled, anethole helps to loosen and clear out mucus and debris that clog the nasal passages, providing instant relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and improved airflow. As a result, fennel decoctions provide natural and effective relief from nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Fennel's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can help to alleviate pressure and discomfort associated with congestion.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, further contributing to congestion.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene that helps with nasal congestion by acting as a bronchodilator, which helps to relax the muscles in the airways and improve breathing.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin is a phenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and promoting clear breathing.
Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole is a terpene that has natural decongestant properties, helping to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby alleviating congestion.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make decoctions for nasal congestion due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make decoctions for nasal congestion due to their expectorant and mucolytic properties.
Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make decoctions for nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning when they are at their most potent.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare into small pieces to release their bioactive compounds quickly.
Combine the chopped foeniculum vulgare with 1l of boiling water to create a decoction that takes 10-15 minutes to prepare.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction steep for 5-7 minutes to maximize the release of their active properties.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a clear liquid that is ready to use as is.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Treetop hydrangea decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction contains flavonoids and terpenes that work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the decoction's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, providing quick relief from stuffiness and sinus pressure. By addressing both inflammation and congestion, treetop hydrangea decoctions offer a natural and effective solution for alleviating nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: The decoction contains high amounts of mucilage, a thick, gel-like substance that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, throat, and sinuses, reducing inflammation and irritation.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Treetop Hydrangea contains bioactive compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and constriction of nasal passages and sinus cavities.

Expectorant action: The decoction's saponins help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and breathe more easily through congested airways.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful flavonoid antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating congestion.
Phenolic acids: The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of phenolic acids in Hydrangea arborescens decoctions help combat infections and reduce nasal congestion.
Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin's anti-inflammatory properties and ability to block histamine release help alleviate nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make decoctions due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages.
Leaves: Utilized in decoctions for their expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the nasal cavities.
Flowers: Employed in decoctions for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat infections and reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hydrangea arborescens roots in the fall when the plant is dormant for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for 2 to 3 weeks to remove moisture.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and measure out 1 to 2 teaspoons per cup of water.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 cup of boiling water and steep for 5 to 10 minutes to create decoction.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids to obtain a concentrated herbal liquid for medicinal use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural diuretic properties, which help to thin and flush out excess mucus from the nasal passages. The antioxidants present in dandelion also soothe and calm inflamed tissues, reducing swelling and redness. Additionally, dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease sinus pressure and promote drainage, allowing for easier breathing and relief from congestion. By promoting healthy mucus production and clearance, dandelion decoctions provide a natural remedy for relieving nasal congestion and associated symptoms.
remedy_properties: Diuretic property: Dandelion decoction's diuretic property helps to reduce sinus pressure and congestion by increasing urine production, which in turn reduces excess mucus and fluid buildup in the nasal passages.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion decoction help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal tissues, allowing for easier breathing and relieving congestion symptoms.
Expectorant property: Dandelion's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing nasal congestion.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene ester acts as a decongestant by reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, thereby relieving congestion.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These phenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Fatty acids (e.g., linoleic acid, oleic acid): The anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties of these unsaturated fatty acids help to break down mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their diuretic properties, helping to thin mucus and promote nasal discharge.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with cold water to remove any dirt.
Chop the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 tablespoon of chopped taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 1 cup of boiling water to create a decoction.
Simmer the taraxacum officinale decoction for 5-7 minutes to release its active compounds and flavors.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease constriction in the nasal passages. This allows for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness. Additionally, ginger's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus, further reducing congestion. By soothing and opening up the airways, herbal ginger decoctions provide a natural and effective solution for alleviating nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Expectorant properties: Ginger has natural expectorant properties, meaning it helps loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier drainage.

Antioxidants and antibacterial compounds: Ginger contains antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that help combat infections and reduce oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can contribute to congestion.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These iridoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease nasal congestion.
Shogaols: These compounds have decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to break down mucus and promote its expulsion from the nasal passages.
Beta-sesquiphellandrene: This terpene has bronchodilatory effects, which help relax the muscles in the airways and improve airflow, making it easier to breathe and alleviate nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain active compounds that help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Buds: Used in decoctions due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help ease nasal congestion and cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 inches of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the early morning when moisture content is lowest.
Peel the rhizomes using a vegetable peeler and slice them into thin 1/8-inch thick rounds.
Combine 1 teaspoon of the sliced rhizome with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reduces by half and reaches desired consistency.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black pepper decoctions helps with nasal congestion because it contains compounds that have natural decongestant properties. The active ingredient, piperine, works by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, black pepper's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and reduce swelling in the nasal tissues. By drinking a decoction of black pepper, you can experience quick relief from sinus pressure and congestion, allowing you to breathe freely again.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Black pepper helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing and reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory property: Black pepper's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure.
Antimicrobial property: Black pepper's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause nasal congestion, such as the common cold or sinusitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in piperine, a bioactive compound that helps to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages.
Rhyzomes: Contain piperonal, an essential oil with decongestant properties that can help to alleviate nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried black pepper powder into a small saucepan.
Add two cups of distilled water to the saucepan with the black pepper powder.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat and then reduce the heat to low.
Simmer the decoction for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Coriander decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural expectorant properties that loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages. The active compounds in coriander, such as cineole and linolool, have a warming effect on the mucous membranes, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation caused by colds, allergies, or sinus infections. As a result, coriander decoctions provide relief from nasal congestion, allowing for easier breathing and improved oxygenation of the body.
remedy_properties: Decongestant properties: Coriander's decoction helps to reduce nasal congestion by dilating blood vessels and reducing swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing.

Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties: Coriander's decoction exhibits antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat infections causing nasal congestion and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, promoting healing and relief from discomfort.

Expectorant properties: Coriander's decoction stimulates the production of mucus, helping to loosen and clear out excess mucus and debris from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Coriandrum sativum are commonly used to make decoctions that help relieve nasal congestion due to their warming and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Coriander seeds have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including nasal congestion, thanks to their carminative and decongestant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh coriander leaves and stems or use 1 teaspoon dried powder.
Combine the ingredients with 2 cups boiling water in a saucepan and reduce heat to simmer.
Steep for 10-15 minutes allowing the mixture to infuse its medicinal properties into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit at room temperature for 30-60 minutes before drinking as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The apigenin and bisabolol present in chamomile flowers have been shown to reduce swelling and ease constriction in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects calm irritated mucous membranes, helping to thin out mucus and promote its expulsion from the body. By addressing these underlying factors, chamomile decoctions provide effective relief from nasal congestion, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
Mucolytic properties: The mucolytic agents in chamomile, like alpha-bisabolol, break down excess mucus, allowing it to be easily cleared from the nasal passages and relieving congestion.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants help protect the nasal tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by irritants, further reducing inflammation and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of chamomile are used to treat nasal congestion due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for relieving nasal congestion as they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes the mucous membranes in the nose and sinuses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one tablespoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers.
Measure out 250 milliliters of boiling water and pour it over the herbs in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds from the flowers.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool slightly before consuming it as a hot liquid within an hour of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The essential oils present in rosemary, such as camphor and borneol, have been shown to help ease respiratory issues by reducing inflammation and thinning mucus. When consumed as a decoction, these compounds work together to break down and clear out excess mucus, providing fast relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for those who are prone to recurring nasal congestion or sensitive to over-the-counter decongestants.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary contains anti-inflammatory compounds like camphor, borneol, and bornyl acetate, which help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and alleviate congestion.
Decongestant effects: The essential oil of rosemary has been shown to have decongestant properties, which can help shrink swollen blood vessels in the nose and relieve nasal congestion.

Antioxidant activity: Rosemary decoctions rich in antioxidants can help protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative damage caused by pollutants, allergens, and other irritants, which can contribute to chronic congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of rosemary have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including nasal congestion, due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Seeds: Rosemary seeds contain compounds like camphene and borneol, which help to loosen mucus and ease congestion in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried rosemary leaves measure one teaspoon of dried herbs per cup boiling water.
Combine the dried rosemary and boiling water steep for five minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve discard solids retaining liquid.
Transfer the decoction to a glass container store in refrigerator up to three days.
Take one-quarter cup of the cooled decoction as needed to relieve nasal congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural ability to ease respiratory tract inflammation. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of lemon balm, a member of the mint family, work together to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, allowing for easier breathing. As the warm decoction is inhaled, it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing fast and effective relief from congestion and sinus pressure. This natural remedy promotes healthy sinuses and eases discomfort caused by colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Eucalyptol: This property helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from congestion.
Citral: Citral has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to congestion.
Rosmarinic acid: This antioxidant-rich compound has natural decongestant properties that can help reduce swelling and ease breathing by constricting blood vessels in the nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lemon balm roots can help soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain a high concentration of camphor, which helps to thin mucus and ease congestion.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a clean glass bowl.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the herb and let it steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container.
Discard the solids and store the liquid in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two days.
Take one quarter cup of the decoction three times daily, warm or at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the nasal passages and sinuses. The valerian root contains compounds that have a direct effect on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and relieving congestion. This makes it an effective natural remedy for sinus pressure, stuffiness, and runny nose. Additionally, valerian's calming effects can also help reduce stress and promote better sleep, further aiding in the relief of nasal congestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like effects: Valerian contains compounds that mimic the action of antihistamines, which helps to relieve nasal congestion by reducing inflammation and constriction of blood vessels in the nose.
Expectorant properties: Valerian's saponins and essential oils help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract and providing relief from nasal congestion.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are used because they contain a high concentration of volatile oils that help to thin mucus and ease congestion.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, making it easier for mucus to drain.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried valeriana officinalis root for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured valeriana officinalis root with filtered boiling water in a medium saucepan.
Simmer the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes to release the active compounds and essential oils.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a large glass measuring cup.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. The volatile oils present in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing and sinus drainage. Additionally, the calming effects of English lavender can help to relax the nasal tissues, further alleviating congestion and promoting a sense of relief from stuffy noses.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lavender decoction helps to reduce histamine release, which can alleviate nasal congestion caused by allergic reactions or sinusitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, providing relief from congestion.
Expectorant properties: Lavender decoction's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it effective in reducing nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried l avandula angustifolia flowers and transfer them to a heat-resistant glass container.
Combine the lavender flowers with 4 cups of distilled water in a medium saucepan and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce the heat to low and let the decoction simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to steep and release their active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid extract.
Store the herbal decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days and consume 1/2 cup 2-3 times daily as needed to alleviate nasal congestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active compound, berberine, has been shown to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe through congested airways. Additionally, goldenseal's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses helps to combat infections that can cause sinus pressure and congestion. By reducing inflammation and fighting off pathogens, goldenseal decoctions provide natural relief from nasal congestion and related symptoms.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and combat infections that can cause congestion.
Berbamine: Possesses antiviral and antibacterial properties, which help to alleviate congestion by reducing the severity of colds and flu, and preventing bacterial overgrowth.
Alkaloids: Have expectorant and mucolytic properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of goldenseal contain berberine, a natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to relieve nasal congestion by reducing swelling and fighting off infections.
Barks: The barks of goldenseal contain berberine and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder per eight ounces of water.
Bring the water to a boil in a pot and add the goldenseal root powder.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Let the decoction cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea decoctions helps with nasal congestion because they contain natural decongestants, anti-inflammatory agents, and expectorants that work together to relieve sinus pressure and congestion. The steam from hot tea can also help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the bioactive compounds in herbs such as eucalyptus, peppermint, and ginger can reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease breathing, providing fast and effective relief from stuffy noses and sinuses.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Many herbs, such as thyme, eucalyptus, and peppermint, contain compounds that help loosen and thin mucus, making it easier to expel from the body.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like ginger, turmeric, and elderflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, helping to alleviate congestion.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbs such as echinacea, goldenseal, and yarrow have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections causing nasal congestion by killing or inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of the tea plant contain flavonoids and tannins, which help to shrink swollen mucous membranes and reduce excess mucus production in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried camellia sinensis leaves for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a medium-sized pot and add cold filtered water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or cup.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking and inhaling the steam.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural decongestant properties. The plant's essential oils, such as citral and linalool, work to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, lemon verbena's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out mucus, further alleviating congestion. As a result, this natural remedy can provide effective relief from nasal congestion without the need for harsh medications or chemical-based products.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Lemon verbena decoctions help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel congested airways.
Antihistaminic properties: The herb contains compounds that inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that causes blood vessels to swell and lead to increased nasal congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena decoctions contain flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to congested airways.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of the lemon verbena plant contain compounds that help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers, rinsing them thoroughly with clean water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried or three teaspoons of fresh herb with one cup of boiling distilled water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Add one tablespoon of raw honey and one pinch of sea salt to enhance flavor and benefits.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before drinking two to three cups daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herbs in the decoction, such as Hibiscus sabdariffa and Rosa rugosa, contain flavonoids and anthocyanins that help to reduce swelling and ease sinus pressure. When consumed as a warm tea, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and travel to the nasal passages, where they help to thin mucus and promote drainage, providing relief from congestion and sinus headaches.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: The decoction helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal blend reduces inflammation in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure.
Antihistamine-like properties: The decoction's natural compounds help to reduce histamine release, which is often responsible for nasal congestion, runny nose, and sneezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to break down mucus and reduce congestion in the nasal passages.
Bark: Contains flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and relieve pressure in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried rosa x rugosa flowers and green leaves measuring one tablespoon per cup of water.
Combine the herb mixture with boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Allow the decoction to steep for 10-15 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids.
Drink one to two cups of the warm herbal decoction three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic decoctions helps with nasal congestion because it has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the nasal passages. The decoction's active compounds, such as allicin and sulfur-containing amino acids, have anti-inflammatory effects that reduce swelling in the nasal tissues and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, garlic's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can cause congestion, promoting a healthy respiratory system and alleviating symptoms of colds and allergies.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Garlic's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal passages, which can contribute to congestion.
Expectorant properties: Garlic's saponins help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic's active compounds, such as allicin, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Garlic roots have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating nasal congestion by reducing swelling and combating infections.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems (rhyzomes) of garlic contain sulfur compounds, which have natural decongestant properties that can help relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried allium sativum bulbs from a reputable source for optimal efficacy.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried bulb powder and place it in a medium-sized heatproof cup.
Pour boiling water over the bulb powder, allowing it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean teapot or mug, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool slightly before consuming in small sips throughout the day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages. The antioxidants present in yarrow also reduce inflammation in the nose and sinuses, making it easier for the body to breathe and expel excess mucus. Additionally, yarrow's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling and congestion in the nasal tissues. As a result, yarrow decoctions can provide effective relief from stuffy noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat infections and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion.

Expectorant properties: Yarrow's expectorant properties stimulate the production of mucus and facilitate its expulsion from the lungs and sinuses, helping to clear out excess mucus and relieve congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier for air to pass through and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, yarrow roots have anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, making them effective in relieving nasal congestion.
Barks: Yarrow barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and promote drainage, providing relief from stuffy noses.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves and stems measuring one tablespoon per quart of water.
Combine the harvested plant material with cold water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture over low flame for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a simmering point.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds, including allantoin and rosmarinic acid, which work synergistically to reduce swelling and ease constriction in the nasal passages. As a result, comfrey decoctions can help thin and clear mucus, allowing for easier breathing and relief from stuffiness. Additionally, its expectorant properties help to loosen and eliminate excess phlegm, providing long-lasting relief from sinus pressure and congestion.
remedy_properties: Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Comfrey decoction helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages, thereby relieving congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal tissues, which can contribute to congestion and sinus pressure.

Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Comfrey decoction has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause respiratory infections, including those that lead to nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of comfrey contain a high concentration of rosmarinic acid, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages.

Barks: Comfrey barks contain a wealth of flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective at combating sinus infections and reducing nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried symphytum officinale herb one part by weight and pure filtered water two parts by volume.
Combine the dried herb and water in a pot bringing to a boil then reducing heat.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes allowing the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and refrigerate for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Barberry decoctions helps with nasal congestion because its active compounds, berberine and isoquinoline alkaloids, possess natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages, break down mucus, and combat infection-causing pathogens that can exacerbate congestion. By soothing and clearing the sinuses, barberry decoctions can provide quick and effective relief from stuffy noses, allowing for easier breathing and a clearer sense of smell and taste.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Barberry decoction reduces inflammation in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and relieve symptoms of rhinitis and sinusitis.
Expectorant properties: The decoction loosens and clears mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion caused by colds, allergies, or bronchitis.

Antiviral properties: Barberry's antiviral activity helps to combat viral infections that cause nasal congestion, such as the common cold or flu, by inhibiting the growth of viruses and preventing their replication in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids, such as berberine, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help alleviate nasal congestion.
Rhizomes: Containing high levels of essential oils and sesquiterpenes, these underground stems have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues like nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried berberis vulagaris root powder in a cup.
Combine the rinsed powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean cup, discarding solids.
Add two to three tablespoons of honey to sweeten and flavor the decoction as desired.
Allow the decoction to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature before consuming within 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn decoctions helps with nasal congestion because its unique blend of flavonoids, triterpenes, and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, hawthorn's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, further alleviating congestion. By soothing and calming the respiratory system, hawthorn decoctions provide natural relief from nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and discomfort.
Antibacterial and antiviral properties: Hawthorn's antimicrobial agents can combat infections that cause nasal congestion, preventing the growth of bacteria and viruses that exacerbate congestion.
Expectorant properties: Hawthorn's saponins and flavonoids help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel excess mucus and relieve nasal congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, Hawthorn roots help to reduce inflammation and soothe nasal passages.
Barks: The astringent properties of Hawthorn barks can help to dry up excess mucus and reduce congestion.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried crataegus monogyna leaves with filtered water to remove impurities.
Combine 20 grams of the rinsed leaves with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes to allow the mixture to steep and release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of 39 degrees fahrenheit for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mistletoe decoctions helps with nasal congestion because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The decoction's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear out mucus, making it easier for your body to expel. Additionally, mistletoe contains compounds that help to relax the mucous membranes, allowing them to produce thinner, easier-to-clear mucus. This combination of anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties makes mistletoe decoctions a natural and effective way to alleviate nasal congestion.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Mistletoe's antiviral compounds, such as viscotoxins, can help combat viral infections that cause nasal congestion by inhibiting the replication of viruses and reducing inflammation.

Expectorant properties: The saponins and flavonoids present in mistletoe decoctions have a natural expectorant effect, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and other debris from the nasal passages, providing relief from congestion.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Mistletoe's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as phenolic acids, can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal tissues, making it easier to breathe and relieving sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of mistletoe contain alkaloids and flavonoids that help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion in the nasal passages.
Buds: Mistletoe buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing nasal irritation and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh viscum album leaves measuring two tablespoons for each cup of water needed.
Rinse the harvested viscum album leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove impurities.
Combine the rinsed viscum album leaves with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10 minutes of cooling.
Store the prepared decoction in an airtight container at room temperature for up to three days or refrigerate for up to seven days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with nasal congestion because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the nose. The decoction's expectorant qualities help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, reducing sinus pressure and congestion. Additionally, plantain's natural antiseptic properties may help to reduce swelling and redness in the nasal passages, promoting a healthier respiratory system and ease of breathing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain decoctions contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, alleviating congestion and sinus pressure.
Antihistamine-like properties: The saponins present in plantain decoctions can help block histamine receptors, reducing the excess mucus production and relieving nasal congestion.
Expectorant properties: Plantain's mucilage content helps loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and clear out congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
Barks: Containing astringent compounds that constrict blood vessels and reduce nasal discharge.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried plantago major leaves from a reputable supplier.
Rinse the plant material in cold water to remove any debris or contaminants.
Boil one liter of distilled water in a stainless steel pot and add the plantago major.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the decoction has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and store refrigerated.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nasal congestion that are treated with herbal decoctions are various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. Herbal decoctions can also alleviate sinusitis, pharyngitis, and laryngitis symptoms like inflammation, pain, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, decoctions may be used to treat gastrointestinal issues like digestive discomfort, diarrhea, and dysentery, as well as skin problems like eczema and acne.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with nasal congestion is a blend of eucalyptus, peppermint, and thyme. Eucalyptus oil's decongestant properties help ease stuffiness, while peppermint's menthol reduces inflammation and opens airways. Thyme's antimicrobial properties combat underlying infections, and its expectorant properties aid in mucous clearance. This trio can be combined in a steam inhalation or as a warm drink to provide relief from nasal congestion and promote a healthy respiratory system. This natural remedy can be a soothing alternative to over-the-counter medications.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Turkish archives of otorhinolaryngology, herbal decoctions are beneficial for nasal congestion due to their effectiveness in treating acute postviral rhinosinusitis (APRS). The study, which included 46 APRS patients, found that patients who received a herbal preparation containing extracts of five medicinal plants (Sinulan forte<sup>®</sup>) experienced significant improvements in nasal congestion symptoms. Specifically, the absolute post-treatment scores for nasal congestion were lower in patients receiving the herbal preparation, suggesting that herbal decoctions can be a valuable treatment option for nasal congestion.
